docs.title: services.pcsd.enable
docs.text:
Whether to enable pcsd.

Type: boolean

Default: false

Example: true

Declared by: - modules/default.nix#L41

docs.title: services.pcsd.enableBinaryCache
docs.text:
Option to add the binary cache to your settings.

Type: boolean

Default: false

Declared by: - modules/default.nix#L43

docs.title: services.pcsd.enableWebUI
docs.text:
Enable the webUI of pcsd.

Type: boolean

Default: false

Declared by: - modules/default.nix#L144

docs.title: services.pcsd.package
docs.text:
The pcs package to use. By default, this option will use the packages.default as exposed by this flake.

Type: package

Default: pcsd.packages.x86_64-linux.default

Declared by: - modules/default.nix#L116

docs.title: services.pcsd.clusterName
docs.text:
Name of the cluster. This option will be passed to services.corosync.clusterName.

Type: string

Default: "nixcluster"

Declared by: - modules/default.nix#L60

docs.title: services.pcsd.clusterUserPasswordFile
docs.text:
Required path to a file containing the password of the hacluster user in clear text.

Type: null or path

Declared by: - modules/default.nix#L162

docs.title: services.pcsd.corosyncKeyFile
docs.text:
Required path to a file containing the key for corosync. See corosync-keygen(8).

Type: null or path

Declared by: - modules/default.nix#L52

docs.title: services.pcsd.extraCommands
docs.text:
A list of additional pcs commands to run after everything else is setup. Cannot have the -f option. See pcs(8)

Type: list of string matching the pattern ^(pcs .*)$

Default: [ ]

Example:

[
  "pcs property set stonith-enabled=false"
]

Declared by: - modules/default.nix#L319

docs.title: services.pcsd.finalPackage
docs.text:
The package defined by services.pcsd.package with overrides applied.

Type: package (read only)

Default:

pcsd.packages.x86_64-linux.default.override {
  pcs-web-ui = pcsd.packages.x86_64-linux.pcs-web-ui;
  withWebUI = false;
}

Declared by: - modules/default.nix#L126

docs.title: services.pcsd.mainNode
docs.text:
The name of the node in charge of updating the cluster settings. Defaults to the first node declared in services.pcsd.nodes.

Type: string

Default: "Name of your first node"

Declared by: - modules/default.nix#L68

docs.title: services.pcsd.nodes
docs.text:
List of nodes in the cluster. This option will be passed to services.corosync.nodelist.

Type: list of (submodule)

Default: [ ]

Example:

[
  {
    name = "this Machine's Hostname";
    nodeid = 1;
    ring_addrs = [
      # This is where your machine's local ips go
      "192.168.0.255"
    ];
  }

  # the other nodes of your cluster go here
]

Declared by: - modules/default.nix#L78

docs.title: services.pcsd.nodes.*.name
docs.text:
Node name.

Type: string

Declared by: - modules/default.nix#L103

docs.title: services.pcsd.nodes.*.nodeid
docs.text:
Node ID number.

Type: signed integer

Declared by: - modules/default.nix#L99

docs.title: services.pcsd.nodes.*.ring_addrs
docs.text:
List of IP addresses, one for each ring.

Type: list of string

Declared by: - modules/default.nix#L107

docs.title: services.pcsd.systemdResources
docs.text:
An attribute set that represents all the systemd services that will be managed by pcsd.

Type: attribute set of (submodule)

Default: { }

Example:

systemdResources = {
  "caddy" = {
    enable = true;
    group = "caddy-grp";
  };

  "headscale" = {
    enable = true;
    group = "caddy-grp";
    startAfter = ["caddy"];
  };
}

Declared by: - modules/default.nix#L169

docs.title: services.pcsd.systemdResources.<name>.enable
docs.text:
Whether this service is managed by pcs or not. If not enabled, this service can only be started by a user manually.

Type: boolean

Default: true

Declared by: - modules/default.nix#L191

docs.title: services.pcsd.systemdResources.<name>.extraArgs
docs.text:
Additional command line options added to pcs commands when making a systemd resource.

Type: list of string

Default: [ ]

Declared by: - modules/default.nix#L231

docs.title: services.pcsd.systemdResources.<name>.group
docs.text:
The name of the group in which we want to place this resource. This allows multiple resources to always be on the same node and can also make the order in which the resources start configurable.

Type: null or string

Default: null

Declared by: - modules/default.nix#L209

docs.title: services.pcsd.systemdResources.<name>.startAfter
docs.text:
Determines what resources need to be started after this one. Requires a group. Can only be the name of resources in the same group and cannot be the name of this ressource.

Type: list of string

Default: [ ]

Declared by: - modules/default.nix#L219

docs.title: services.pcsd.systemdResources.<name>.startBefore
docs.text:
Determines what resources need to be started before this one. Requires a group. Can only be the name of resources in the same group and cannot be the name of this ressource.

Type: list of string

Default: [ ]

Declared by: - modules/default.nix#L225

docs.title: services.pcsd.systemdResources.<name>.systemdName
docs.text:
The name of the systemd unit file without ‘.service’. By default, this option will use the name of this attribute.

Type: string

Default: "‹name›"

Declared by: - modules/default.nix#L200

docs.title: services.pcsd.virtualIps
docs.text:
An attribute set that represents all the virtual IPs that will be managed by pcsd.

Type: attribute set of (submodule)

Default: { }

Example:

virtualIps = {
  "caddy-vip" = {
    ip = "10.0.0.130";
    interface = "eno1";
    group = "caddy-grp";
    startBefore = ["caddy"];
  };
}

Declared by: - modules/default.nix#L242

docs.title: services.pcsd.virtualIps.<name>.cidr
docs.text:
The CIDR range of the IP.

Type: signed integer

Default: 24

Declared by: - modules/default.nix#L280

docs.title: services.pcsd.virtualIps.<name>.extraArgs
docs.text:
Additional command line options added to pcs commands when making a virtual IP.

Type: list of string

Default: [ ]

Declared by: - modules/default.nix#L308

docs.title: services.pcsd.virtualIps.<name>.group
docs.text:
The name of the group in which we want to place this resource. This allows multiple resources to always be on the same node and can also make the order in which the resources start configurable.

Type: null or string

Default: null

Declared by: - modules/default.nix#L286

docs.title: services.pcsd.virtualIps.<name>.id
docs.text:
The name of the resource as pacemaker sees it. By default, this option will use the name of this attribute.

Type: string

Default: "‹name›"

Declared by: - modules/default.nix#L260

docs.title: services.pcsd.virtualIps.<name>.interface
docs.text:
The network interface this IP will be bound to.

Type: string

Default: "eno1"

Declared by: - modules/default.nix#L269

docs.title: services.pcsd.virtualIps.<name>.ip
docs.text:
The actual IP address.

Type: string

Declared by: - modules/default.nix#L275

docs.title: services.pcsd.virtualIps.<name>.startAfter
docs.text:
Determines what resources need to be started after this one. Requires a group. Can only be the name of resources in the same group and cannot be the name of this ressource.

Type: list of string

Default: [ ]

Declared by: - modules/default.nix#L296

docs.title: services.pcsd.virtualIps.<name>.startBefore
docs.text:
Determines what resources need to be started before this one. Requires a group. Can only be the name of resources in the same group and cannot be the name of this ressource.

Type: list of string

Default: [ ]

Declared by: - modules/default.nix#L302

docs.title: services.pcsd.webUIPackage
docs.text:
The pcs webUI package to use. By default, this option will use the packages.pcs-web-ui as exposed by this flake.

Type: package

Default: pcsd.packages.x86_64-linux.pcs-web-ui

Declared by: - modules/default.nix#L152
